tldr: Airbyte is a connector-driven ELT platform with a managed cloud option, while Apache NiFi is an open-source dataflow engine focused on routing, transforming, and tracking data with provenance. Migration means rebuilding pipelines as NiFi flows rather than importing connector configs directly.
why_switch: You need fine-grained data routing and transformation, not just ELT
You want strong data provenance tracking out of the box
You prefer a fully self-hosted Apache-licensed dataflow engine
You need real-time or streaming flows alongside batch loads
You want to avoid per-connector or volume-based subscription pricing
what_to_export: List of all source and destination connectors in use
Stream and table selections per connection
Credentials and secrets stored in Airbyte (rotate during migration)
Custom connectors built with the Airbyte CDK
Operational metadata (sync history, failure rates) for benchmarking
export_method: In Airbyte, document each connection: source type, destination type, sync schedule, stream selection, and any normalization or transformation steps. Use the Airbyte UI or its configuration export feature to capture connector settings, but treat the export as a reference rather than something importable into NiFi — schemas differ. Securely record any source and destination credentials so you can recreate them in NiFi processors; rotate them after the migration. If you built custom connectors with the Airbyte CDK, save the connector code separately. Capture historical sync metrics so you can benchmark NiFi flows against Airbyte performance.
import_method: Install Apache NiFi (self-hosted) on a VM, container, or cluster following the official deployment guide. For each Airbyte connection, build an equivalent NiFi flow using built-in processors: a source processor (database, HTTP, S3, Kafka, etc.) reads data, a chain of processors handles transformation, and a sink processor writes to your destination. Use NiFi's parameter contexts and controller services to manage credentials securely. Schedule flows using the processor scheduling settings rather than a centralized cron. Validate provenance and data lineage in NiFi's UI, then run flows in parallel with Airbyte during a cutover window before turning Airbyte off.
gotchas: NiFi is much lower level than Airbyte; expect to build flows manually rather than configure connectors
Many Airbyte connectors have no exact NiFi equivalent — you may need scripting or custom processors
Operating NiFi in production requires capacity planning, clustering, and backup of the flow.json file
Schedules and incremental sync semantics differ; verify cursors and state handling per flow
Provenance data can grow quickly; configure repository sizes to prevent disk pressure
Security model is different (NiFi uses certificates and policies); plan SSO and access controls early

email.subject: Canceling our Airbyte subscription in 14 days
email.body:
Hi team,

We are migrating our data pipelines from Airbyte to a self-hosted Apache NiFi setup. Please treat this email as 14 days notice to cancel our Airbyte subscription. Kindly confirm the end date, any final invoice, and steps to deactivate access. Thank you for the support during onboarding.

Best,
Your customer
